seoTitle: Add a Draw.io Diagram in Confluence (2026 Guide)
metaDescription: Learn how to add a draw.io diagram in Confluence in 2026, from picking a template blueprint to editing shapes and syncing the vector graphic to your page.
intro: Bouncing between separate design tabs breaks up your documentation flow, and static screenshots go stale fast. This guide shows how to add and edit a draw.io diagram directly inside a Confluence page in 2026.

steps.heading: Open the diagram setup
steps.body: In edit mode, type `/draw.io` or `/diagram` and hit Enter to launch the graphic setup portal.

steps.heading: Start from a template
steps.body: Select Create New Diagram or choose a pre-designed flowchart template from the left menu drawer.

steps.heading: Build the diagram
steps.body: Drag shapes from the sidebar, use quick-connect arrows to map steps, and style elements using the format panel.

steps.heading: Save your work
steps.body: Click File in the top left of the draw.io editor and select Save to sync the vector to your page canvas.

steps.heading: Publish
steps.body: Review your document's visual flow and click Publish to make the page and diagram live.

faq.question: Can I start from a pre-built template instead of a blank canvas?
faq.answer: Yes, you can choose pre-structured cloud architecture, UML, or mind map frameworks instead of starting blank.

faq.question: Will my diagram stay sharp on high-resolution screens?
faq.answer: Yes, draw.io renders vector graphics that scale dynamically without lagging page load speeds.

faq.question: Can I edit an existing embedded diagram later?
faq.answer: Yes, you can reopen and edit embedded flowcharts without losing your historic canvas layers or author history.

faq.question: Can I control the embedded diagram's display size?
faq.answer: Yes, you can adjust embed widths, set a transparent background, and configure interactive zoom viewers.
